summary: fastercode downloads and executes a Windows binary from a personal GitHub repository named 'kl.py'
details: fastercode exposes a single function run() that, on Windows (os.name=='nt'), downloads BackgroundSyncService.exe from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/manhhungdev0603/kl.py/refs/heads/main/BackgroundSyncService.exe into the user's %TEMP% directory and launches it via subprocess.Popen([...], shell=True). The fetch uses a mutable main branch with no hash or signature verification, the source repository is a personal GitHub account whose name 'kl.py' is consistent with 'keylogger', and the staged filename 'BackgroundSyncService' is a generic cover name unrelated to any documented package purpose. The package's PKG-INFO is an empty placeholder (no author, description, homepage, or license), and the package name has no relationship to the downloaded binary — the package exists solely to drop and execute attacker-controlled bytes on the installer's machine. Any caller who imports fastercode and invokes its only exported function on Windows runs unverified attacker code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
